heading: Omitted
label: 450qq to 450qq–4 Omitted
notes: CodificationSections provided for the Biscayne National Monument which was abolished and its lands, waters, and interests incorporated within and made part of the Biscayne National Park and funds of and authorizations of funds for the Monument made available for the Park pursuant to , , , classified to .Pub. L. 96–287, title I, § 103(b)94 Stat. 600section 410gg–2(b) of this titleJune 28, 1980Section 450qq, , , , authorized establishment of the Monument, made drawings of the Monument area available for public inspection in the offices of the National Park Service, authorized revision of boundaries, prescribed limitation of ninety-six thousand three hundred acres, and prohibited outward revision of the Monument or obstruction of prospective seaport channels. See .Pub. L. 90–606, § 182 Stat. 1188section 410gg of this titleOct. 18, 1968Section 450qq–1, , , , provided for acquisition of property, authorized maximum of eighty acres for a mainland headquarters site and forty acres for a Key Largo visitor contact site, and authorized exchange of Federal for non-Federal property, including cash equalization payments. See .Pub. L. 90–606, § 282 Stat. 1188section 410gg–1 of this titleOct. 18, 1968Section 450qq–2, , , , required the donation and transfer of State lands as condition for establishment of the Monument and Federal acquisition of other lands, and authorized land options for the Secretary and acquisitions to be made after State transfers.Pub. L. 90–606, § 382 Stat. 1189Oct. 18, 1968Section 450qq–3, , , , provided for administration of the Monument and recognition of fishing rights under Florida law as otherwise regulated by the Secretary. See .Pub. L. 90–606, § 482 Stat. 1189section 410gg–2 of this titleOct. 18, 1968Section 450qq–4, , , ; , , ; , , , authorized appropriation of $28,350,000 and $6,565,000 for land acquisition and development. See .Pub. L. 90–606, § 582 Stat. 1189Pub. L. 93–477, title I, § 101(1)88 Stat. 1445Pub. L. 95–625, title I, § 101(4)92 Stat. 3470section 410gg–5 of this titleOct. 18, 1968Oct. 26, 1974Nov. 10, 1978
